---
name: RFC — Request for Comments
about: Propose a significant architectural or product change for community discussion.
labels: kind/rfc, status/open-for-comment
assignees: ''
---

<!--
RFCs are for substantial, cross-cutting, or architecturally significant changes.
For small bug fixes or minor features, open a regular issue or PR instead.

An RFC is not a PR. It is a structured proposal for discussion. Code comes later.
The Technical Council reviews all open RFCs every 2 weeks.
-->

## RFC Title

<!-- A short, precise title. -->

## Status

`Draft` — open for community comment

<!-- Lifecycle: Draft → Under TC Review → Accepted / Deferred / Rejected -->

## Summary

<!-- 2–3 sentences. What are you proposing and why? -->

## Motivation

<!-- What problem does this solve? Why does ArizenOS need this?
     Include: current limitations, user pain points, missing capabilities.
     Be specific. Link to related issues. -->

## Detailed Design

<!-- The technical meat of the proposal. Include:
     - Architecture changes
     - New APIs or modified APIs (with signatures if possible)
     - Data structures and formats
     - Interaction with existing modules
     - Performance considerations
     - Security implications -->

### API Changes

```typescript
// Proposed new API or changed API signatures
```

### Module Interactions

<!-- Which modules are affected? How do they interact with this change? -->

### Migration Path

<!-- If this changes existing behavior, how do current users/extensions migrate? -->

## Drawbacks

<!-- Honest assessment: what are the downsides of this proposal?
     What does it make harder? What does it break? What does it cost? -->

## Alternatives

<!-- What other approaches were considered? Why is this one better?
     Include the option of doing nothing and explain why that's worse. -->

## Unresolved Questions

<!-- List open questions the community should weigh in on.
     These are what the RFC discussion should focus on. -->

1.
2.
3.

## Implementation Plan

<!-- High-level plan: who does what, in what order, over what timeline?
     Does not need to be precise at RFC stage. -->

- [ ] Phase 1:
- [ ] Phase 2:
- [ ] Phase 3:
